description_long: This is a medium-sized, arched gate with a robust stone frame and a vertically paneled wooden door. The frame is polygonal and appears to be constructed from gray stone blocks, some of which have subtle, irregular surface markings for a natural look. The wooden door is composed of several vertical planks, tightly fitted together, and is set flush within the stone archway. The gate lacks any visible handles, hinges, or decorative hardware, giving it a utilitarian and sturdy appearance. The back of the gate mirrors the front, with the same stone and wood construction, and the sides reveal the thickness of the stone frame. This type of gate would be suitable for use in urban parks, alleys, or as an access point in a stone wall or perimeter barrier.
description: A sturdy arched gate with a gray stone frame and vertical wooden planks, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched stone frame and vertical wooden planks of the gate.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the stone frame, with no visible door details.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with the same stone arch and wooden planks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the stone frame's thickness and lack of door details.
